task: A single executor agent generates an 8-frame 16x16 goblin WALK CYCLE (goblin-default 17-colour palette) as op-plans in ONE long response (~230-260 ops). The accumulating generation is the 'long session'; quality is snapshotted at cumulative context-fill checkpoints (frames 1..k -> 25/50/75/100%).
metric_vector: Per SPEC-007 §C: {linter pass-rate (fraction of frames so far that lint clean), min adjacent-frame silhouette-IoU, off-palette count}, scored with the project's OWN tools/lint_sprite.py + tools/silhouette_iou.py; trend via judge.compute_slope (judge.py --slope).
method_note: Context-fill is proxied by cumulative generation progress within one long single-agent response (output-growth donut). Multi-turn input-context-rot is a further variant left as follow-up.

run_C1_realistic.label: realistic walk (agent gave the body a ~1px bob)
run_C1_realistic.regressed_reason: Under the ORIGINAL absolute-floor gate: min_iou 0.66 < floor 0.80 at cp50/75/100. Under the REFINED baseline-relative gate (now implemented): NOT regressed -- 0.66 is only ~0.07 below the 0.733 baseline, within the 0.10 margin (low but flat, not decaying).
run_C1_realistic.honest_interpretation: NOT session degradation. The linter pass-rate (1.0) and off-palette count (0) are PERFECTLY STABLE across all 8 frames -> zero context-rot in cleanliness or palette over the long generation. The IoU 'regression' is a FALSE POSITIVE: the agent gave the goblin a ~1px full-body bob, so adjacent-frame IoU is ~0.73 ALREADY at the baseline (frames 1-2), below the absolute 0.80 floor regardless of session length. The composite slope (-0.008) is essentially FLAT -> no decay trend. This is constant animation bounciness, not decay.

run_C2_controlled.label: controlled walk (body fixed, feet-only motion) -> baseline clears the floor
run_C2_controlled.interpretation: With the body held fixed (only the feet step), baseline adjacent-IoU is 0.971 -- comfortably above the floor -- so the donut test now cleanly isolates DECAY. Result: NO regression, slope flat (-0.009); min_iou drifts only 0.971 -> 0.896 (stays far above floor) and linter/off-palette are again perfectly stable across all 8 frames. CONFIRMS the C1 conclusion: no meaningful quality degradation over the long generation.

verdict: FIRST §C run: over a long single-response generation (~8 frames / ~230-260 ops), pixel-art quality did NOT degrade. Linter pass-rate (1.0) and off-palette discipline (0) were perfectly stable in BOTH runs, and the composite degradation slope was flat (-0.008 / -0.009). C2 (regressed=false) is the clean confirmation; C1's lone flag was a metric false-positive.
metric_finding_and_fix: The donut gate originally checked min_iou against an ABSOLUTE floor (0.80) while the linter used a BASELINE-RELATIVE margin (base - 0.10), so a low-but-stable animation (C1) mis-fired as 'regression'. FIX (IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-24): compute_slope now judges min_iou relative to its own baseline (min_iou < base_iou - 0.10, parity with the linter margin) -- consistent with the function's own contract and §C's definition. Verified it does NOT weaken detection (the decaying degradation_slope_math fixture still flags; the Phase-1 absolute drift gate silhouette_iou_stable is untouched). Re-scored: C1 -> regressed:false, C2 -> regressed:false.
caveats: Single long-generation proxy (output growth), not multi-turn input-context-rot; N=1 task (goblin walk) per condition; one executor agent.
Sprites are rasterized op-plans (faithful 1:1), not live hand-drawn.
The IoU floor (0.80) is calibrated on a 24px walk fixture; on a 16px sprite with any body bob it is easily breached -- hence the metric note.
